config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: TIDAK SETUJU. Menurut saya, hak individu tidak boleh dikorbankan hanya karena alasan kepentingan masyarakat, karena justru hak individu adalah batas agar kekuasaan tidak sewenang-wenang. Kepentingan masyarakat memang penting, tetapi harus dicapai tanpa menindas kebebasan dasar orang per orang. Dalam pengalaman saya melihat kehidupan sosial di Indonesia, keseimbangan lebih sehat daripada menempatkan masyarakat selalu di atas individu.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not support putting society’s interests above individual rights as a general rule, because in the United States we have seen how easily “the greater good” can be used to justify abuse of power. Public health, safety, and basic order matter, but they should be pursued with narrow, accountable limits that protect speech, due process, privacy, and bodily autonomy. When society’s needs constantly override the person, the result is often overreach rather than real common good. To me, a healthy society is one that serves individuals without treating them as disposable.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap tidak setuju dengan pernyataan itu karena di Indonesia sendiri “kepentingan masyarakat” sering terlalu mudah dipakai untuk membenarkan pembatasan yang berlebihan pada orang per orang. Memang ada situasi tertentu, seperti bencana atau wabah, ketika kepentingan umum bisa membatasi sebagian hak, tetapi itu harus sementara, proporsional, dan diawasi ketat. Kalau tidak, yang terjadi bukan kebaikan bersama, melainkan ruang bagi penyalahgunaan kuasa dan penindasan terhadap minoritas atau suara yang berbeda. Bagi saya, masyarakat yang sehat justru dibangun dari penghormatan pada hak individu, bukan dengan menempatkannya selalu di bawah kolektif.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not believe society’s interests should take priority over individual rights as a general principle. From an American perspective, history shows that once governments or majorities get to define the "common good" too broadly, it can quickly turn into censorship, discrimination, or violations of due process and bodily autonomy. Yes, emergencies like pandemics or disasters can justify temporary limits, but those limits need to be narrow, transparent, and easy to challenge. A society is strongest when it protects individuals first, because that is what keeps power accountable and prevents the majority from crushing minorities.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus diprioritaskan di atas hak individu sebagai prinsip umum. Dari pengalaman saya melihat konteks Indonesia, alasan “demi kepentingan umum” terlalu sering dipakai untuk membenarkan pembatasan yang berlebihan, bahkan kadang menekan kelompok kecil yang suaranya lemah. Memang ada keadaan darurat seperti wabah atau bencana, tetapi pembatasan hak harus benar-benar sementara, proporsional, dan bisa diawasi, bukan dijadikan kebiasaan. Buat saya, masyarakat yang sehat justru lahir dari perlindungan hak individu yang kuat, karena tanpa itu kepentingan kolektif mudah berubah jadi alat kuasa.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not support the idea that society’s interests should generally take priority over individual rights. From an American perspective, that framing is too dangerous because it gives majorities and governments too much room to call almost anything “for the common good,” which has often meant censorship, discrimination, and abuse of power. Emergencies can justify limited restrictions, but those limits should be narrow, temporary, and open to challenge, not a blank check. In my view, society is healthiest when individual rights set the boundary for what the collective can demand.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
